video_title: Mob War: Philadelphia vs. The Mafia | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: This mini series was a let down. I grew up in the Philadelphia area through the 70's, 80's and 90's. This program 'fast forwarded' through the  background of the Philly mob through the 70's and early 80's - critical information to the Stanfa/Merlino conflict - in just the first 10 minutes. Then the balance of the first 2 of 3 episodes presented the Stanfa/Merlino conflict in good detail, but 2nd episode ended with the arrest and convictions of both Stanfa and Merlino. It was the end of the 'Mob War'. But there's a 3rd episode that profiles the FBI trying to pinch Merlino on a RICO charge. There's no Stanfa, there's no 'war'. The 3rd episode was a waste. This topic had the potential to be a good series on the mob in Philadelphia, poor production prevented that from happening.

comment_text: Fast forward to the present day, and Joey Merlino is a beloved figure on social media. Athletes from every sport, musicians from every genre and actors all love Joey, and he's arguably bigger now than he's ever been. The reason he's a massive star is because Joey walked the walk and talked the talk. To this day, he'll look right at you and say the mob doesn't exist. Joey never flipped, never ratted, and did his time without a peep. Other mobsters talked a big game but ran into the arms of the FBI when things got heavy. Not Joey, he lived and breathed the mafia tenets.
